AssumptionRef.description: Stable reference to an assumption ledger entry. Carried in `CompatibilityProof.assumptions` and rendered in the UI's AssumptionCard.

AuditorProcedure.description: How an authorized auditor can retrieve the suppressed content. Tagged so the UI branches on retrieval kind rather than free text.

oneOf.description: File a ticket; the restricted-archive operator retrieves content. `ticket_template` is a stable identifier (URL, queue name) the UI can deep-link to.
Permanently deleted; only the deletion-authority reference remains. `deletion_id` lets auditors confirm the deletion in the operator's deletion log.
In-process retrieval via an authenticated API endpoint. `scope` names the OAuth/JWT scope required.

ChainOfCustody.description: Required chain-of-custody fields on every provenance record. Composed as `Option<ChainOfCustody>` on record types so older records without custody info remain valid.

auditor_access.description: How an authorized auditor retrieves full content.

cryptographic_commitment.description: Cryptographic commitment (BLAKE3 hash) over the suppressed payload. `None` when the payload is structurally guessable from a hash and a non-public commitment is filed under the restricted-archive procedure.

policy_rule_id.description: Foreign key into the `policy_rules:` registry in `config/assumption-policy.yaml`. Constructed via [`PolicyRuleId::new`] in registry-aware contexts, or via [`PolicyRuleId::unchecked`] (the `From<&str>` / `From<String>` blanket impls) where the registry isn't reachable.

suppression_class.description: Class of suppression / redaction applied (matches a registered taxonomy ID, e.g. `phi_strict`, `prompt_injection`, `controlled_access_genotype`).

CompatibilityProof.description: Proof that a producer port satisfies a consumer port. The compatibility engine produces these; they carry adapter steps, assumption references, and policy decisions inline.

assumptions.description: Assumption ledger entries this edge depends on. Populated by the planner when type or facet compatibility is uncertain.

evidence.description: Supporting evidence (registry snapshot ids, validator outputs, etc.). Used for replay determinism tests.

facet_matches.description: Per-facet match decisions (modality, organism, genome build, etc.). Empty for ports with no rich facets.

format_conversions.description: Format conversions performed (e.g. `gzip` → ungzipped). Non-empty when adapters are inserted between producer and consumer.

inserted_adapter_node_ids.description: Adapter task node ids inserted between producer and consumer. One id per adapter step in chain order.

ontology_subsumption_path.description: Ontology subsumption path the engine traversed (parent IRIs from producer to consumer). Empty when types are identical.

policy_decisions.description: Policy decisions recorded by the compatibility engine. E.g. `phi_propagation_allowed` or `clinical_gate_passed`.

required_validators.description: Validators that must pass before execution. Resolved by the claim_verifier at runtime.

warnings.description: Warnings worth surfacing in the UI but not blocking.

ContentCommitment.description: BLAKE3 commitment over the suppressed payload. The algorithm name is preserved verbatim in the record so future curators can add alternate algorithms without ambiguity at read time.

algorithm.description: Algorithm name (always `"blake3"` for records produced by `ChainOfCustody::with_commitment`).

digest_hex.description: Hex-encoded digest. BLAKE3 produces 32 bytes → 64 hex chars.

FacetMatch.description: Per-facet match decision in a `CompatibilityProof`. Stable shape so UI rendering is consistent across facets.

producer.description: Producer's value (free-form because facet types are heterogeneous).

rationale.description: Optional rationale (e.g. "GRCh37 → GRCh38 via UCSC liftover").

oneOf.description: Producer and consumer values are identical.
Producer is a subtype of consumer (e.g. "Homo sapiens" of "mammal").
Producer was substituted to match consumer (e.g. liftover). Implies an adapter was inserted.
Engine could not decide; assumption ledger entry created.

ProofEvidence.description: Supporting evidence for a `CompatibilityProof`. Stable enough to replay in determinism tests.

oneOf.description: Registry snapshot id (atom registry, ontology version).
Validator output reference. Resolved at provenance-emit time.
Free-form citation (e.g. EDAM term URI + version).

SuppressionClass.description: Closed taxonomy of suppression kinds. Maps to the §15.2 policy table and to ingestion-safety quarantine reasons.

oneOf.description: PHI strict — patient identifiers, dates of birth, etc.
Structurally guessable identifier (e.g. short numeric MRN) for which a plain hash would still leak information; the commitment is filed restricted instead of inlined.
Raw credential captured incidentally (API key, password).
User-supplied free text whose redaction status is unknown.
Proprietary pipeline detail an operator opted out of publishing.

ValidatorRef.description: Reference to a validator implementation. Resolved by the harness verify endpoint against `claim_extractor` + `claim_verifier` and numeric/structural validators.

parameters.description: Optional inline parameters (e.g. {"max_p": 1.0}). Schema is validator-specific; the composer treats this as opaque.

items.description: One edge in a `WorkflowDag`. Connects a producer port to a consumer port and carries the compatibility proof or report explaining why the edge exists.

chain_of_custody.description: Chain-of-custody for the edge when one or both endpoints carry suppressed content. `None` for ordinary non-suppressed edges. Older records without the field deserialize cleanly.

from_port.description: Producer port name (must be a member of the producer's `outputs`).

proof.description: Compatibility proof. Carried inline so consumers don't need a side lookup to render "why this edge exists."

to_port.description: Consumer port name (must be a member of the consumer's `inputs`).

items.title: EdgeContract
